R. Nova da Piedade 62, 1200-299 Lisboa, Portugal
review:
Bar Alimentar fue sin duda, una de las mejores comidas que tuvimos en Lisboa. Todo lo que pedimos estuvo espectacular, ingredientes de super alta calidad, sabores muy bien equilibrados y platos originales sin sentirse pretenciosos. Se nota el cuidado en cada detalle y el ambiente hace que quieras quedarte toda la noche.

Pero lo que realmente hizo la diferencia fue Eugenia. Nos atendió de una forma increíble!  Fue cálida, atenta, simpática y con recomendaciones excelentes. Ese tipo de servicio que hace que la experiencia pase de ser muy buena a inolvidable.

Si están en Lisboa, este lugar es una parada obligatoria. Ya entiendo por qué tanta gente lo recomienda. Volvería sin pensarlo.

address: R. Nova da Piedade 62, 1200-299 Lisboa, Portugal
R. Nova da Piedade 62, 1200-299 Lisboa, Portugal
review:
10/10 for the venue. We were seated on the terrace, and the setting was absolutely magical. Even on a very warm day, the temperature was surprisingly comfortable and made for a perfect dining experience.

10/10 for the food as well. Everything we tried was outstanding, from the delicious snacks and shared starters to the fish, pasta dishes, and especially the beef tongue sandwich, which was absolutely exceptional.

The service was warm, professional, and genuinely friendly throughout. The only small point I’d mention concerns the table timing. I completely understand the need to turn tables and respect reservation slots, especially in a busy restaurant. However, we were asked about wrapping up our meal while a couple of dishes we had ordered had still not arrived, including one that we had specifically reordered because we loved it so much.

In the end, the team handled the situation well, the dish arrived, and we were able to enjoy it before leaving. As someone who also works in hospitality, I know how challenging these situations can be. My only suggestion would be to ensure that the pace of service and the table timing remain perfectly aligned, so guests never feel rushed while still waiting for food.

That said, this was a fantastic experience overall. We had a wonderful lunch, enjoyed several bottles of wine, and left with great memories. I can’t wait to return with friends on my next trip to Lisbon.

review:
My favorite meal in my trip to Lisbon this May.  A lively dining room and beautiful backyard, we wish we could have dined back there!

The signature cocktails were very impressive, and they recommended a great off menu bottle of wine following that.

The food showed a great level of detail in the preparation, but the restaurant still had an unpretentious vibe about it.  Standouts were the asparagus risotto, octopus, oysters, and the croquettes.  Additionally, the prices were reasonable for the quality of food and service.

Highly recommend getting a reservation here if you can!

review: Já havia frequentado o Bar Alimentar algumas vezes e sempre sai satisfeito, apesar das porções serem bem pequenas o sabor é bastante bom. Porém na última sexta-feira ao voltar com um grupo de 8 amigos fomos pressionados o tempo todo pelas atendentes para que fizéssemos logo os pedidos. Erraram os pratos, o vinho branco estava quente e ao final de 2 horas trouxeram nos a conta sem termos pedido. Ao pedirmos sobremesas e café fomos tratados com má educação pela atendente de cabelo alourado que saiu e voltou com talheres de sobremesa e jogou-os todos sobre a mesa, de maneira rude e mal educada, como se estivéssemos a fazer algo de errado. Foi pedir autorização para nos servir. O café também igualmente mal servido numa taça de água, para além de estar frio… Foi uma situação bastante embaraçosa. Uma mesa de 8 pessoas onde cada um  pagou 56€ por um atendimento que desmerece o nível do restaurante. Eu gostava de la ir, mas depois deste episódio não pretendo lá voltar.

review:
Bar Alimentar was high up on my list for restaurants I wanted to visit when visiting Lisbon. We had a mixed experience.

The service was great, everyone was attentive and knowledgeable so we felt looked after. The restaurant also has a cute vibe with an awesome playlist!

Food for us was hit or miss. Some dishes were unbelievable like the crudo, beef tartare polenta, mussels on toast, and desserts but then we found the ravioli cold and lacking flavour, the octopus dish was okay but barely had any octopus in it.

Overall, worth trying out for some great drinks and small plates. Might be a strong appertivo spot before heading out for a full dinner.

address: R. Nova da Piedade 62, 1200-299 Lisboa, Portugal
review:
A conveyor belt, not a restaurant. Don't get fooled by the silent videos and the "only candles" atmosphere - it's packed, you are elbow to elbow at the tables and it's EXTREMLY noisy. There is no romantic quiet time here.

The most off putting thing is actually the treatment - I get that it's a popular place, however I highly doubt that the "shift-system" is actually in place to sit more people, but rather to make more money. Once you enter you are immediately told "i need the table in 2 hours on the dot" cuz they'll will sit other people. I would've disregarded it, had they not, 10 mins before, cleared our table without us having finished our desserts and drinks! Before you ask how can someone take your food - my partner was alone at the table, one server was at one of his sides with the bill and the other server cleared the table from the other side...

We were sat at the only drafty table, which was VERY uncomfortable. Upon seating, there was another free table, but we were told it was for three people - it was not. Later 2 people sat there. Once two tables were free and we hadn't started eating yet, we kindly asked if we could get moved and were denied our request.

Yes, the food is good, although it's a pricey place with smaller portions, but it's not worth it for the feeling of being rushed or mistreated.

(The second star for service is only because NOT OUR server kept noticing that we need wine refills....)

address: R. Nova da Piedade 62, 1200-299 Lisboa, Portugal
review:
I had a truly delicious dinner there. The cannoli with codfish are outstanding. It is the perfect combination of Sicilian specialty with a Portuguese twist. You know it sweet but this time it is savory.
We also enjoyed a lot the polenta, the asparagus risotto with the little slices of confit lemon, and the polvo dish.
The chocolate mousse with the salty caramel and the crunchy hazelnut were so good.
Since it is popular, you need to book and the table is reserved for only a certain time. But everything is very efficient, so that the time allocated is enough. The place is very nicely decorated.
I want to come back and try the rest of the menu.

review:
Let me start by saying the food was pretty good.
I've been working in the service industry for 10+ years and am currently working at a Michelin star restaurant in Chicago.
Service was welcoming and we had a reservation for 10pm, once we were sat (at a VERY small table for 3 people) in a backroom/patio which felt like walking into a 1980s Italian restaurant (front/main dining room + bar was way cooler and more modern hipste/ vibey)
the service seemed rush which was very different from every other place I've been to in Portugal
We ordered and got all but our food but the last main within 20 minutes with no room on the table for more than 2 dishes at a time and SAs aggressively trying to take plates away to make space for everything else. (Just coming out way too fast)
Couldn't tell if the server was super inexperienced and didn't understand how to course things out or just didn't care.
At one point that server was replaced by another one who was way more professional and we ended up getting free shots along with another table next to us and enjoy a delicious dinner overall was good but definitely ruined the experience as a whole even though it was stabilized at the end

review:
Thank you so much for your understanding — we have resolved this misunderstanding in the best way.
I am very grateful to Marcio, the manager, for the quick response to my review and for the kind assistance in clarifying everything.
I also truly appreciated the help in choosing the wine, which made my experience even more special.
On my next trip to Lisbon, I will definitely come back, and I can warmly recommend this place to others as well.

The evening before
I came to this bar hoping to enjoy my first evening in Lisbon with good cocktails and a pleasant atmosphere. Unfortunately, it turned into one of the worst experiences I’ve ever had while traveling.

After ordering, I paid with a €200 bill. The bartender gave me change as if I had only handed over €20. When I immediately pointed out the mistake, he denied it, insisted that I was wrong, and even suggested calling the police. To make matters worse, he tore up my receipt right in front of me, leaving me without any proof of the transaction.
response_of_owner: Dear Xena - my name is Marcio and I’m in charge of this project and after reading your message I was (am!) in shock for its a very serious matter that you have described. I wish you to kindly accept before all my immediate apologies on behalf of this situation (note: I am not nor was at the bar while this happened) and all that it matters to Machimbombo Bar is that you may come back and receive 200€ in hand! From a management level this is our only and minimum way to make sure this situation may be resolved and (very unfortunately) not erased . We have CCTV cameras which allow us to clarify this situation.

review: Honestly torn about this review. On the one hand, the vibes were great and the drinks were solid. On the other, the bartender who seemed cold at first, seemed to warm up to my fiancée and I after a couple drinks, and he then made us several drinks we didn’t order (the priciest ones of course). It seemed as though he genuinely wanted us to try them and see what we thought. At the end of the night they were all on our bill which should have been 50 euros but ended up being over 100. Despite our complaints he insisted we ordered them. We felt taken advantage of, which ruined the experience.
This is such a fun bar with unique drinks/flavors. Definitely worth checking out in Lisbon. Music is a vibe as well.

Praia da Ursa — Portugal’s Most Stunning Wild Beach!
If you are looking for a place that feels like a prehistoric movie set, this is it. Praia da Ursa (Bear Beach) is a true hidden gem of the Atlantic coast, located just a stone's throw away from Cabo da Roca. It is not just a place to sunbathe; it is a visual masterpiece and a raw experience of nature that you will remember for the rest of your life. The massive jagged rock formations rising out of the crashing Atlantic waves and the golden sand create an atmosphere of absolute freedom.
This place is a must-visit for any nature lover, but it requires serious preparation.
Essential Tips for Your Visit:
Footwear is Key: Forget about flip-flops or sandals. The descent to the beach is steep, rocky, and can be quite slippery with loose gravel. Wear sturdy sneakers or hiking boots. The hike down takes about 20–30 minutes, and the climb back up can be physically demanding.
Pack Everything You Need: There is zero infrastructure here—no cafes, no toilets, and no umbrellas. Be sure to bring plenty of water, snacks, and sunscreen. You are completely on your own once you reach the sand.
Check the Tides: During high tide, a large portion of the beach disappears under the water. I highly recommend checking a tide chart before you go so you have enough space to walk around the iconic rocks.
Water Temperature & Currents: The Atlantic is freezing here even in the summer, and the currents are incredibly powerful and unpredictable. Swimming is only for very experienced swimmers and should be done with extreme caution. It is also often very windy, so a light windbreaker in your bag is a good idea.
Timing is Everything: The best time to visit is 2–3 hours before sunset. The light becomes magical, and the rocks cast dramatic shadows. However, make sure to start your hike back up before it gets completely dark, as the trail is not lit and can be dangerous at night.
The journey here was totally worth it! Be sure to bring water, sunscreen, and something to snack on.

review: Absolutely, the crackle-glazed white tiles and that sweeping wave-like roof make it one of the most photogenic spots in Lisbon. place_name: MAAT - Museum of Art, Architecture and Technology
review:
Came for the future of art, architecture, and technology… stayed for a masterclass in prehistoric ventilation systems.

The building is stunning — like a smooth sci-fi wave casually chilling by the river. You walk in expecting cutting-edge innovation, bold ideas, maybe even a robot whispering existential truths into your ear. And honestly, the exhibitions and architecture mostly deliver.

But then… you enter the bathroom.

Suddenly it’s less “Museum of Art, Architecture and Technology” and more “Escape Room: Sauna Edition.” The air is hot, thick, mysterious, and possibly sentient. You start wondering if this is actually an immersive installation about climate change, or just a bold architectural experiment answering the question: what if airflow simply didn’t exist?

It’s genuinely impressive how a place dedicated to technology managed to invent a bathroom that feels like it predates it.

And just when you recover, you show up for the 17:00 guided tour… at 17:15 still no guide, no explanation, just you and a growing group of people collectively questioning the concept of time. Maybe this is the “technology” part — a live experiment in relativity.

That said, the museum itself is great — beautiful space, cool exhibitions, amazing views. Just maybe visit the bathroom before you go, or treat it as a high-stakes endurance performance piece.

review:
MAAT를 가기 위해서 트램을 타고 이동했는데 MAAT로 가는 길은 생각보다 좁고 정류장도 작아서 잘 찾아야됩니다. 심지어 저희는 트램 벨을 눌렀는데 기사님이 그냥 지나치셔서 두정류장을 지나쳤다가 다시 트램을 타고 와서 내릴 수 있었습니다. 리스보아카드가 없없다면, 교통비를 두번 내야되는 상황이었습니다.
어렵게 방문한 MAAT의 갤러리 지붕 풍경은 정말 아름다웠습니다. Santuário de Cristo Rei 구세주 그리스도상이 정말 가깝게 보였습니다. 예전 화력발전소를 재활용해서 만든 전시관 답게 화력발전소의 모습도 볼 수 있었으며, 다양한 예술작품 및 오케스트라 공연까지 볼 수 있었던 멋진 경험을 했습니다.

This is hands down the best viewpoint in all of Lisbon. Because it is the highest point in the city, the panoramic views of the castle, the river, and the colorful rooftops are completely unobstructed. We went just before sunset, and the atmosphere was magical with live acoustic music playing. It does get quite crowded, and the walk up the hill is a serious workout, but the reward at the top is 100% worth it.

review: The Miradouro de São Pedro de Alcântara viewpoint provided one of the best panoramas in Lisbon. From its elevated position, we enjoyed sweeping views of the city’s red-tiled rooftops, historic neighborhoods, and São Jorge Castle perched on a distant hill. The beautifully landscaped terrace offered a perfect place to pause and take in the scenery while appreciating Lisbon’s unique layout of hills and winding streets. It was an ideal spot for photos and a memorable way to see the city from above.

Está junto a un bonito parque. Es uno de los monumentos más famosos de Lisboa y forma un conjunto temático de la época de los grandes descubrimientos portugueses junto con el Monasterio de los Jerónimos (ss.XV-XVI) y el moderno Monumento a los Descubrimientos (s.XX).

A must if you are in Lisbon. Make sure to book your time online specially if you are visiting during the peak season like we were. We bought the Lisbon City Pass and this is included to visit as “free”. There are canyons on display at the ground floor and the rest of it is just an open space but no doubt that it has great architecture and its white facade is very stunning to see in person. They control the crowds getting in but in my experience they opened it 15 minutes even before the scheduled slot so be early if you want to be first.

卡爾莫修道院～卡爾莫修道院（Convento do Carmo）與內部的卡爾莫考古博物館（Museu Arqueológico do Carmo）是里斯本必訪的地標。無頂的哥特式拱樑在藍天下矗立，是 1755 年大地震最具代表性的見證，館內則典藏了珍貴的古羅馬石碑與埃及木乃伊。

Il Convento do Carmo è stata una delle visite più suggestive e affascinanti del mio viaggio a Lisbona. Le imponenti rovine della chiesa, con le arcate gotiche rimaste aperte verso il cielo, creano un'atmosfera davvero unica e suggestiva. Camminare tra queste mura è un'esperienza particolare, capace di trasmettere allo stesso tempo bellezza, storia e una certa emozione.
Il museo ospitato all'interno è molto interessante e permette di approfondire la storia del convento e della città, con reperti e testimonianze davvero affascinanti. La visita è ben organizzata e offre un'esperienza culturale piacevole e coinvolgente.
Consiglio assolutamente il Convento do Carmo a chiunque visiti Lisbona: è un luogo ricco di fascino, diverso dai monumenti più tradizionali e davvero indimenticabile. Una delle visite che ho apprezzato di più durante il viaggio.

A Carmo kolostor (portugálul: Convento do Carmo) Lisszabon egyik legkülönlegesebb és történelmileg jelentős műemléke, amely az 1755-ös nagy lisszaboni földrengés emlékeként áll. A kolostor romjai a város történelmét és az események hatását mesélik el, miközben lenyűgöző példát nyújtanak a gótikus építészeti stílusra. A kolostort 1389-ben alapította Nuno Álvares Pereira, aki a portugál történelem egyik legfontosabb hadvezére és vallási alakja volt.
A Szent Kármeli Rend szerzetesei számára épült, és évszázadokon át a spirituális élet központja volt Lisszabonban.
Az 1755-ös földrengés során a kolostor jelentős része összeomlott. Bár egyes részeket helyreállítottak, a tető nélküli hajót szándékosan hagyták romos állapotban, hogy emléket állítson a természeti katasztrófának.

The Carmo Archaeological Museum is absolutely worth stopping at while you’re in Lisbon. The first thing that hit us was the skeleton of the old Gothic arches… just standing completely open to the sky. It’s a strange and beautiful sight, especially with the city moving around you, just outside the ruins. The museum itself isn’t huge, but that actually worked well for us. It’s peaceful, thoughtfully laid out, and you can comfortably see everything in around 30 minutes or so without feeling rushed at all. Between the ruins, the preserved artifacts, and the quiet atmosphere, it’s a great place to slow down for a bit and take in some history. The €7 entrance fee felt completely fair for the experience. Definitely recommend adding it to your Lisbon itinerary. Cheers!

Здесь буквально ощущается история: величественные готические арки без крыши, пережившие разрушительное землетрясение 1755 года, создают особую атмосферу. Это место, где архитектура, инженерное мастерство и память о прошлом соединяются под открытым небом. Обязательно к посещению. ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐

review: After waiting in line for about 15 minutes, we rode the Santa Justa Lift, one of Lisbon’s most iconic landmarks. The historic elevator, which opened in 1902, connects the lower Baixa district with the higher Carmo neighborhood. Its unique ironwork design and fascinating history made it one of the highlights of our visit to the city.

review:
The Santa Justa Lift is one of the most iconic landmarks in Lisbon, combining unique architecture with stunning panoramic views. This historic iron elevator connects the lower streets of Baixa with the higher Carmo district and has been a symbol of the city for over a century.

The ride to the top is short but memorable, and the observation deck offers fantastic views of Lisbon’s rooftops, the Tagus River, and the historic city centre. The intricate ironwork and Neo-Gothic design make the lift just as impressive as the views it provides.

It can get busy, especially during peak tourist hours, but the experience is well worth it.

review:
Pink Street, znana również jako Rua Nova do Carvalho, to miejsce, które w ostatnich latach zdobyło popularność dzięki influencerom, którzy zachwalają je jako "konieczność" podczas wizyty w Lizbonie. Po przyjeździe do tego rzekomo hipnotyzującego zakątka, nie mogę oprzeć się wrażeniu, że to bardziej marketingowa iluzja niż prawdziwa atrakcja.

Jakie było moje zaskoczenie, gdy po dotarciu okazało się, że ulica liczy sobie zaledwie 100 metrów i, na pierwszy rzut oka, może wydawać się urokliwa dzięki różowej nawierzchni i kolorowym parasolom. Jednak to, co miało być magicznym doświadczeniem, szybko zamienia się w przygnębiające spostrzeżenie. Różowa farba, choć fotogeniczna, nie wystarczała, żebyśmy zostali tu na dłużej.

W okolicy nie ma żadnych wyjątkowych kawiarni, barów czy sklepów, które mogłyby wzbogacić wizytę. Większość lokali jest raczej przeciętna, a atmosfera przypomina bardziej turystyczną pułapkę niż autentyczne doświadczenie kulinarne czy kulturowe. Dodatkowo byliśmy tam w porze obiadowej a we wszystkich restauracjach wzdłuż ulicy nie było ani jednej osoby, wyglądało to tak jak by dopiero pracownicy restauracji sprzątali po poprzednim dniu/wieczorze.
Oprócz tego, widok kolorowych parasoli i różowej ulicy zaklucili mi pracownicy pobliskich restauracji, którzy ciągnęli wielkie czarne wory ze śmieciami z których ciągnęła się mokra ciecz. Wydaje mi się, że takie sytuacje nie powinny mieć miejsca. Zazwyczaj restauracje mają dodatkowe tylne wejścia, służące do tego a nie wejscie główne, gdzie chodzą turyści.
Masa turystów, którzy przybywają tu, aby zrobić zdjęcie, dodają do miejsca chaosu, co sprawia, że trudno poczuć się swobodnie. Po kilku minutach spaceru po Pink Street, czujesz, że to miejsce nie ma nic więcej do zaoferowania poza chwilą na kilka zdjęć kolorowych parasoli.

Podsumowując, Pink Street to zdecydowanie przereklamowana atrakcja Lizbony. Jeśli planujesz wizytę, lepiej poszukać ciekawszych miejsc w mieście, które oferują autentyczne doświadczenia. Różowa ulica może wyglądać ładnie na zdjęciach, ale w rzeczywistości to tylko krótki odcinek, który łatwo można pominąć.

review: Perched on a hill overlooking Lisbon, São Jorge Castle is one of the city's most iconic landmarks. Originally built by the Moors in the 11th century, the fortress played a key role in defending Lisbon and was later expanded after the city was captured by Portuguese forces in 1147. From its ancient walls and towers, visitors can enjoy spectacular views of Lisbon’s red-tiled rooftops, the Tagus River, and the surrounding neighborhoods. Walking through the castle grounds offers a fascinating glimpse into the city's long and layered history, making it one of the highlights of any visit to Lisbon.

Es un edificio monumental que, con toda razón, está incluido en el Patrimonio de la Humanidad. Su enorme claustro pétreo combina una arquitectura colosal y una preciosa decoración escultórica que incluye figuras humanas, motivos navales y monstruos marinos y dragones. Aunque la entrada no es precisamente barata, merece mucho la pena su visita.
El estilo arquitectónico es precioso por dentro y por fuera, pero es una visita breve, bastante.

Décevant. Le monastère est très beau mais seule une petite partie se visite et il n'y a aucune explication. Même si vous réservez à l'avance, vous ferez environ 30min de queue en plein soleil. Comme nous avions réservé le dernier créneau, le temps d'attente nous a privé de la visite de l'église (on nous a refusé l'entrée  à 17hpile.)

Le billet d'entrée est élevé en comparaison d'autres monastères bien plus intéressants et riches comme celui d'Alcobaça. Envisagez de ne visiter que l'église dont l'entrée est gratuite.
Wunderschön!  Es lohnt sich auf jeden Fall

20 euro pour 15 minutes de "visite". Oui, le bâtiment en lui-même est très jolie, mais payer ce prix pour faire le tour de la place centrale du bâtiment (étage 1 et sol) sans voir de pièce, d'oeuvre d'art ou sans panneaux d'informations, une belle déception.

Nous avons attendu pour réussir à acheter les billets avec une heure précise et encore là, la file pour entrer était gigantesque. Personnellement, attendre au soleil ne me dérange pas, mais cela peut en décourager plusieurs.

Le site internet pour l'achat des billets est zéro intuitif et le kiosque n'est pas à côté du bâtiment donc il faut le chercher et le trouver pour finalement ce faire dire qu'il n'y a plus de place avant 3h de temps. Option A : achat des billets à l'avance. Option B : prendre le risque de ne pas y aller.

Nous avons attendu et nous avons été déçu.

Heureusement, l'entrée pour l'église est gratuite et, selon nous, vos plus la peine que la bâtiment lui-même.

Wird ja für einen Besuch empfohlen, aber ob es wirklich sein muss, weiß ich nicht. Man besichtigt den Innenhof, ein, zwei Räume und das war es auch schon im großen und ganzen. Wir hatten die lisboa card und daher genutzt, aber für den reinen Eintrittspreis hätten wir es uns gespart.

1. 建議要先在網路上買票，買09:30，人最少，排隊的太陽也比較不曬
2. 會有兩個排隊隊伍，上面有寫入場時間的，就是根據買票的時間，進到庭院看建築。
3. 我買09:30進場，09:15排隊，大約09:50才會真的進入庭院。
庭院有兩層樓，無法走回頭路，但其實裡面能參觀的房間不多，認真聽語音導覽和看裝飾，大約1小時出頭就綽綽有餘。
廁所在庭院一樓、出口前。
4. 另一個排隊隊伍，是可以進教堂，屬於免費參觀。但無法從庭院走過去，而是要從庭院出來後，再重新排一次隊入場。我大約早上11點排隊，約20分鐘進入教堂。教堂內容物不多，大約15分鐘可以看完。
5. 若是有買語音導覽，其實也有講解教堂的內容。一開始以為是在修道院沒有逛到，不知道語音導覽在介紹什麼，工作人員也搞不清楚狀況，後來才搞清楚。
6. 整體來說，票價蠻貴的，官方事前的文字導覽也介紹說可以逛到3小時，以為很大、有很多東西可看。但實際上逛，覺得是很棒的建築沒錯，但一下就逛完，感受不太到價值（不知道跟在整修是不是有關，若隔壁棟也有開放，可能會豐富許多）
Un monumento digno de ver
Great place

Il Mosteiro dos Jerónimos è senza dubbio uno dei luoghi più spettacolari che ho visitato a Lisbona. L’architettura è davvero impressionante, soprattutto per la ricchezza dei dettagli e per lo stile manuelino, caratteristico del Portogallo.
Il chiostro è la parte che mi ha colpito di più: elegante, suggestivo e decorato con una quantità incredibile di particolari. Passeggiare tra le sue arcate dà una sensazione particolare e permette di apprezzare pienamente la bellezza e la maestosità del monastero.
Anche l’interno della chiesa è molto affascinante, con le sue grandi colonne e l’atmosfera solenne. La visita può essere un po’ affollata, soprattutto negli orari più turistici, ma vale assolutamente la pena.
Un monumento magnifico e imperdibile per chi visita Lisbona, perfetto per immergersi nella storia e nell’arte del Portogallo.

Be very careful when booking your tickets.  My credit card didn’t go through, so I tried it again.  Evidently it went through and I was double charged.  The website did not indicate that it was working on the transaction. It looked like it froze.
And after several emails, the monastery will not refund one of the transactions. So now I’m stuck with an extra 2 tickets.  I hope it is worth 2 for the price of 4.
This place is amazingly beautiful. Make sure to get your tickets before you go so you don’t have to wait in the long line to purchase.

review:
Le Time Out Market est une sorte de grand marché couvert dédié à la restauration, où l’on retrouve de nombreux stands proposant des spécialités locales et internationales. L’endroit est très populaire auprès des touristes et attire énormément de monde tout au long de la journée.

La qualité de la nourriture est globalement très bonne et il y a un large choix pour tous les goûts. En revanche, il faut s’attendre à une forte affluence : l’ambiance peut rapidement devenir très animée, voire assez bruyante aux heures de pointe. Les prix sont également un peu élevés par rapport à d’autres adresses de Lisbonne, notamment en raison de son succès touristique.

Si vous souhaitez en profiter dans de meilleures conditions, le matin est sans doute le moment le plus agréable pour s’y rendre. L’atmosphère est beaucoup plus calme et c’est l’occasion parfaite pour déguster d’excellents pastéis de nata accompagnés d’un café.

È una piazza molto ampia, probabilmente utilizzata un tempo per il mercato. Si affaccia direttamente sul fiume Tago, talmente grande che sembra il mare. Sul lato interno della piazza, opposto al mare, si apre l'Arco da Rua Augusta, spettacolare porta che dà l'accesso alla zona turistica di ristoranti e negozi vari, il quartiere Baixa, e dove a poche centinaia di metri si trova il famoso Elevator de Santa Julia, una torre con ascensore alta 45 metri che vi trasporta direttamente al Convento do Carmo, una chiesa scoperchiata da un antico terremoto e mai più coperta.
Impressive and worth the trip. Continue through the arch for a wide restaurant choices and plenty of shops.

Esta plaza del Comercio guarda la majestuosidad de la historia de Lisboa. Amplia, rodeada de edificios magníficos que dan realce al espacio. Ubicada frente al río Tajo es el disfrute de sus visitantes.

Hier ist immer was los. Tolle Restaurants und viel Geschichte. Zentral an der Metro.  Hier einen Kaffee oder Snack und dann in die Altstadt, ein Tipp! Von hier auch Bootsfahrten möglich, ebenfalls ein Tipp.
Bien, amplia. Fuimos en final de mundial por lo que estaba la pantalla gigante para ver fútbol.

La Praça do Comércio è una delle piazze più spettacolari e iconiche di Lisbona. La sua enorme ampiezza, gli eleganti edifici gialli che la circondano e la splendida vista sul Tago creano un'atmosfera davvero suggestiva.Passeggiare sotto i portici e attraversare l'imponente Arco da Rua Augusta è un'esperienza molto piacevole, soprattutto al tramonto, quando la luce rende la piazza ancora più affascinante. La posizione è inoltre perfetta per iniziare a esplorare il centro storico di Lisbona e le zone lungo il fiume.Una piazza davvero imperdibile, ricca di storia e di bellezza, che rappresenta perfettamente il fascino monumentale e allo stesso tempo vivace della capitale portoghese. Assolutamente da visitare durante un viaggio a Lisbona!

Visited yesterday with my family (including a 5 year old who had foot surgery the day before) tried to get tickets online to minimise the queue, they didnt except my visa card for obscure reasons, had to use Paypal, paid 92 euro and didn’t get the tickets. Was sent to customer service, they said we had to pay again because the paypal transaction was still pending. Would get my money back when paypal transaction went through. Still waiting for my money back even though I sent all the “paperwork”.

Now we ended up paying 184 euro for a family of four, and a lot of stress and arranging to do in the middle of our holiday.

Aquarium was nice though, unfortunately not the experience we hoped for

Itt a legfinomabb a süti, megéri megkóstolni. A kiszolgálás is gyors, előre vannak csomagolva. Ami miatt 3 csillag, az a minket kiszolgáló dolgozó. Kedves volt, ezzel nem volt baj. Viszont készpénzzel fizettem, és amikor a visszajárót adta volna, megállt a pénztárgépben a keze. Elkezdett oldalra meg hátrafelé nézegetni, közben a keze a pénztárgép pénzes fiókjában pihent, nem mozdult. Aztán valakinek beszélt valamit, vagy csak úgy tett, nem értettem, de még mindig hátrafelé, szemkontaktust kerülve. Húzta az időt, arra játszott, hogy ne kérjem a visszajárót. Nem is kértem el, mondtam, köszönöm, és otthagytam. Akkor megint volt szemkontaktus, és mosoly és elköszönés. Nem volt olyan nagy összeg a visszajáró pénz, nem fog földhöz vágni a hiánya, viszont nekem nagyon úgy tűnik, hogy ez egy jól bevált trükk. Több pénztár van, mögöttünk épp nem állt senki, feltételezem, az alkalmazott eljátszotta már ezt a trükköt néhányszor a turistákkal. Sajnos a kitűnő sütemény mellé egy negatív élmény párosult.

The real deal. I enjoyed visiting over 25 years ago and didn’t realize how much I would miss them. Unfortunately a flight from the states to Lisbon is not in my near term plans and we’ll have to make do with the occasional trip to NJ Portuguese bakeries to get anything close to the original.

I came here because I heard it was the most famous egg tart place in Lisbon, but I have no intention of revisiting, nor would I recommend it. I was extremely offended by the discrimination of one older waiter. I waited in line to eat inside, and when it was my turn to enter, all the staff told me to go further inside. I kept going in until I reached an area with empty tables, but an older-looking male waiter pointed to the exit and spoke to me as if I were being told to leave. I told him I was looking for a table, but he insisted I leave. Then, he pointed to two tables in his zone and told the people who followed me in to sit there. It seems he didn't want me, an asian foreigner, to sit in his area. Portugal is a beautiful country, but it seems some people here do not welcome tourists of certain races.

This is probably the most-reviewed place in Lisbon for Pastéis de Nata. The venue is very large, but the service felt inconsistent. The staff in the garden area weren’t attentive—they didn’t respond or seem interested in helping.

Fortunately, one of the employees inside the main indoor area noticed us, took care of us quickly, and brought our order without delay.

I really liked the custard filling. It was delicious, creamy, and not overly sweet compared to many other pastel de nata places. However, the pastry itself was overbaked, making it too crispy and quite hard, which was the biggest downside for me.

If they could combine the filling from this place with the pastry from some of the other bakeries in Lisbon, it would create the perfect pastel de nata. Overall, I’d say it’s an average place. If they paid more attention to the pastry, it could easily become one of the best, because the filling is genuinely excellent.
Davvero squisiti. Da non perdere, assolutamente i migliori di Lisbona!

O lugar é lindo, cheio de história, rendeu fotos lindas, a decoração do banheiro também é linda. Eu esperava mais da comida!!!

review: This was my second visit to Bessa. On my first visit, I tried the grilled sea bass and a glass of Port wine. The sea bass was excellent. This time, I ordered the shrimp casserole and the paella. The shrimp was delicious, perfectly cooked, and had just the right amount of richness. Unfortunately, it's not easy to find shrimp cooked to the perfect texture, but they got it exactly right here. I chose the chicken paella, and both the chicken and the vegetables were well-balanced and full of flavor. It was served in its original pan, which is always a nice touch. The Port wine was as wonderful as ever and paired beautifully with the meal. The restaurant is small, cozy, and welcoming. I would definitely recommend making a reservation before coming. It is air-conditioned, and the indoor temperature is comfortable. It's the kind of place where you can enjoy a long meal and conversation with friends or family, with a warm atmosphere that reminds me a little of a traditional Aegean-style Turkish tavern. The only thing missing is a seaside location. The ingredients are fresh, the food is delicious, and the atmosphere is genuinely friendly. Overall, it's a place I can wholeheartedly recommend. I'm already looking forward to my next visit so I can finally try the seafood paella.

Le Palais national de Pena est à voir absolument si vous venez à Lisbonne. Le palais est magnifique, surtout vu de l’extérieur avec son architecture et ses couleurs. Les jardins sont également superbes et s’étendent sur plusieurs kilomètres, parfaits pour une belle balade.

Les intérieurs du palais se visitent aussi, mais je les ai trouvés moins impressionnants que l’extérieur et les jardins.

La vieille ville de Sintra est très jolie également et mérite de prendre un peu de temps pour être découverte. Une très belle visite à faire !

佩納宮（Palácio Nacional da Pena）位於葡萄牙辛特拉（Sintra）的山頂，是 19 世紀歐洲浪漫主義建築的代表作，名列葡萄牙七大奇蹟之一，並於 1995 年被聯合國教科文組織列為世界文化遺產。
它以鮮豔的明黃與磚紅配色著稱，童話般的城堡外觀聳立在茂密森林中，是葡萄牙最受歡迎的景點之一。建築起源： 原址為一座 15 世紀的修道院，但在 1755 年里斯本大地震中嚴重受損。19 世紀 40 年代，葡萄牙國王費迪南二世（King Ferdinand II）買下這片廢墟，將其改建為國王與王室的夏宮。
混搭建築風格： 費迪南二世融合了多種建築元素，包括新曼努埃爾式（葡萄牙特有的海洋與航海裝飾）、新摩爾式（伊斯蘭風格拱門與花磚）、新哥德式與新文藝復興式。

Busy place but really beautiful ~ the pictures you can get here are stunning and views.

The exterior, terraces, and surrounding park are beautiful, but the interior experience can be very crowded. Lines to enter the palace move slowly, and once inside, the one‑way route creates congestion where you often move at the pace of the group.

Stepping through the rooms offers an interesting look at how the Portuguese royal family lived before the 1910 revolution. However, the narrow pathways and steady flow of visitors make it difficult to pause for long.

We visited on a Friday afternoon in May (peak season), and it was extremely busy. If you want a calmer experience, I recommend purchasing tickets directly through the Parques de Sintra website and choosing the earliest morning time slot (9:30 AM). Even with timed entry, lines of 30–90 minutes are common during peak season.

Overall, the palace and grounds are stunning, but the interior crowds can affect the experience.

Il Palacio Nacional da Pena è senza dubbio uno dei luoghi più spettacolari e suggestivi che abbia visitato in Portogallo. Il palazzo, con i suoi colori vivaci, l’architettura fiabesca e la posizione immersa nella splendida natura di Sintra, offre panorami davvero memorabili e un’atmosfera unica.Purtroppo, l’esperienza è stata notevolmente penalizzata dalle code, davvero molto lunghe e spesso poco scorrevoli. I tempi di attesa possono essere estenuanti, soprattutto considerando anche gli spostamenti e l'affollamento all'interno del percorso di visita. È un peccato che un luogo così straordinario debba essere vissuto con tanta confusione e con attese così prolungate.
Nel complesso, il palazzo merita assolutamente una visita per la sua bellezza e originalità, ma consiglio di organizzarsi con largo anticipo e di mettere in conto code e grande affollamento. Un luogo magnifico, ma l’organizzazione dell’accesso potrebbe essere decisamente migliorata.
